_guardrails: Never imply Adams was recruited/poached — his side initiated (Warren → McFadden → school); say so plainly. Name schools only with documented reporting attached; St Kentigern's arc is public record with official responses. Point 'why can't communities develop their own' at funding structures/incentives, never at families. Every overrepresentation chart carries a system-pointing annotation (Hokowhitu warning label). Include the counter-ledger at equal volume.

stkentigern_arc.value: St Kentigern College removed from Auckland's 1A First XV competition (Dec 2018) after rival schools threatened a boycott over player recruitment
Agreed to stand down new-to-school players

stkentigern_quote.value: A principal called it the "bad underbelly" of secondary school sport

still_live_2025.value: In 2025, co-ed principals called a proposed boys-school-only competition "deeply unethical"

spinoff_2018.value: The majority of recruits are "of Māori or Pasifika descent, and from low income households"
"the schools are benefitting from these scholarship programmes more than the students"
His phrase for the recruited kid: "hostage to your own talent"

adams_texture_espn.value: Arrived unkempt, had never worn a tie; classmates joked "Who's this murderer?"

hokowhitu_2004.value: Colonial discourse constructed Māori as naturally physical, channeling Māori men toward sport/manual labour and away from academic pathways — the 'natural athlete' myth as a limiting device
hokowhitu_2004.source: Hokowhitu (2004), 'Tackling Māori Masculinity', The Contemporary Pacific 16(2)
hokowhitu_2004.note: Open access — read in full; quote one sentence at a time.

besnier_2012.value: Athletic migration as household economic strategy in a 'hope economy': families invest in an athlete's body; many are moved, few are paid; risk sits with the family
besnier_2012.source: Besnier (2012), 'The athlete's body and the global condition', American Ethnologist 39(3)

marsters_2019.value: The wellbeing bill: family expectation as motivator and weight; injury as identity crisis; low help-seeking among young Pacific male athletes
marsters_2019.note: Open access; qualitative — render as pull-quote stations, not charts.

tokona_te_raki_2023.value: Māori and Pacific students disproportionately placed in low streams/bands; national de-streaming plan
tokona_te_raki_2023.source: Tokona te Raki / Māori Futures Collective — 'Ending Streaming in Aotearoa' + Kōkirihia (2023)

mccreanor_2010.value: Media representations of Māori athletes reproduce Pākehā framings

five_findings: The channeling — sport offered as THE pathway forecloses the others (Hokowhitu).
The sorting — streaming data is 'those left behind', measured (Tokona te Raki).
The hope economy — families carry the long-shot risk; institutions harvest the near-certain surplus (Besnier).
Extraction without governance — the field is brown, the boardroom is not (RNZ study; Enari).
The wellbeing bill — carried quietly, invisible to standard instruments (Marsters).

context_migration.value: Postwar Pasifika labour migration was encouraged for labour needs; Pacific communities were disproportionately targeted in the 1970s 'dawn raids', for which the NZ government later formally apologised
context_migration.note: CONTEXT, NOT CAUSE — one station, no causal claims about Adams.

S1_funnel.fetch: NZR annual reports: registered secondary-school players → First XV → academy → pro contract → All Black

S2_who_governs.fetch: Pasifika share of pro players vs coaches vs boards/execs vs population (2023 census) — RNZ-covered leadership study + NZR reports + Stats NZ
S2_who_governs.source: RNZ study / NZR / Stats NZ 2023 census

S3_sorting.fetch: Stream/band placement by ethnicity — Tokona te Raki / Kōkirihia published figures

S4_timeline.value: Auckland 1A champions by year, annotated REVISIONS-style with the St Kentigern arc (Δ removed → Δ boycott → Δ stand-downs → Δ code) — buildable from public record + coverage set
S4_timeline.source: Public record (Auckland 1A) + St Kentigern coverage set

S5_pre_registered.hypothesis: Elite schools' implicit claim: 'we are where talent must go to develop.' Declare BEFORE pulling data.
S5_pre_registered.method: School attended at 18 (state / integrated / private; home or recruited move) for a defined cohort (All Blacks debutants since 2010 + NZ's NBA players). Report the split whichever way it lands; every athlete's school individually verified or the chart doesn't ship.

data.fetch: Public profiles — each athlete's school-at-18 verified individually
